{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Office Assistant",
  "description": "Starich Recycling Technology is a company focused on innovative solutions for recycling and waste management. the misionis to promote sustainability through advanced recycling technologies as such to reduce waste and environmental impact by creating efficient recycling processes. They provideservices for various materials, including plastics, metals, and electronics.&nbsp;We are into sales of Fiber sheet, Woven bags, Packing belt,Iron rods, Iron nails, Binding wire, Fiber pillows, Matresses e.t.c.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Office Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Alausa, Ikeja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Provides administrative and clerical support to ensure smooth office operations, handling tasks such as scheduling, correspondence, record-keeping, and customer interactions.
	Prepare and edit documents, reports, and presentations.
	Handle office supplies inventory and order supplies as needed.
	Provide administrative support to team members as required.
	Ensure the office environment is tidy and organized.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND with 1-3 years of experience.
	Candidate must be within the age of 25 - 30 years.


Salary
N100,000 - N120,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to:&nbsp;starich.hr@yahoo.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Office Assistant Job at Starich Recycling Technology




        
    

        
        Starich Recycling Technology is a company focused on innovative solutions for recycling and waste management. the misionis to promote sustainability through advanced recycling technologies as such to reduce waste and environmental impact by creating efficient recycling processes. They provideservices for various materials, including plastics, metals, and electronics. We are into sales of Fiber sheet, Woven bags, Packing belt,Iron rods, Iron nails, Binding wire, Fiber pillows, Matresses e.t.c.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Office Assistant

Job Location: Alausa, Ikeja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Provides administrative and clerical support to ensure smooth office operations, handling tasks such as scheduling, correspondence, record-keeping, and customer interactions.
	Prepare and edit documents, reports, and presentations.
	Handle office supplies inventory and order supplies as needed.
	Provide administrative support to team members as required.
	Ensure the office environment is tidy and organized.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree or HND with 1-3 years of experience.
	Candidate must be within the age of 25 - 30 years.


Salary
N100,000 - N120,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Audit Trainee and Digital Marketer",
  "description": "AA Matthew &amp; Co. International Limited was incorporated on the 5th of July 2011 by the Corporate Affairs Commission and is set up as an accountancy and professional services organization with a consistent goal to be recognized as the leading and most trusted professional services firm in Nigeria. We are a part of the AA Matthew &amp; Co Accountancy firm based in the United Kingdom.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Audit Trainee and Digital Marketer
Job Location:&nbsp;Ibeju-Lekki Dangote Refinery, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Assist in servicing local and international accountancy clients.
	Perform bookkeeping using QuickBooks and Excel.
	Conduct bank reconciliations and prepare monthly management accounts.
	Gather and analyse financial paperwork to produce financial reports.
	Assist with audits, tax computations, and corporation tax returns.
	Maintain regular contact with clients and provide support.
	Use social media (Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitter) to promote services and attract clients.(Social media marketing experience is a plus.)
	Social media management (content planning, posting, engagement tracking)
	Social media advert setup and optimisation (Meta, Instagram, etc.)
	Google Ads campaign setup, monitoring, and reporting
	LinkedIn Ads management (B2B targeting and campaign tracking)
	Performance analysis and reporting on digital campaigns.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting or any related discipline.
	1 &ndash; 2 years of experience in an accountancy practice, preferably in a professional services firm.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and QuickBooks is an advantage.
	Must be pursuing a professional certification (e.g., ACCA, ICAN).
	Strong ability to build and maintain professional relationships.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to handle pressure, multitask, and maintain confidentiality.
	Strong computer skills and familiarity with office equipment.


Salary
N140,000 - N150,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to:&nbsp;aamatthewint2@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-01",

  },

Audit Trainee and Digital Marketer Job at AA Matthew & Co. International Limited




        
    

        
        AA Matthew & Co. International Limited was incorporated on the 5th of July 2011 by the Corporate Affairs Commission and is set up as an accountancy and professional services organization with a consistent goal to be recognized as the leading and most trusted professional services firm in Nigeria. We are a part of the AA Matthew & Co Accountancy firm based in the United Kingdom.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Audit Trainee and Digital Marketer
Job Location: Ibeju-Lekki Dangote Refinery, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Assist in servicing local and international accountancy clients.
	Perform bookkeeping using QuickBooks and Excel.
	Conduct bank reconciliations and prepare monthly management accounts.
	Gather and analyse financial paperwork to produce financial reports.
	Assist with audits, tax computations, and corporation tax returns.
	Maintain regular contact with clients and provide support.
	Use social media (Facebook, LinkedIn, Twitter) to promote services and attract clients.(Social media marketing experience is a plus.)
	Social media management (content planning, posting, engagement tracking)
	Social media advert setup and optimisation (Meta, Instagram, etc.)
	Google Ads campaign setup, monitoring, and reporting
	LinkedIn Ads management (B2B targeting and campaign tracking)
	Performance analysis and reporting on digital campaigns.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting or any related discipline.
	1 – 2 years of experience in an accountancy practice, preferably in a professional services firm.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and QuickBooks is an advantage.
	Must be pursuing a professional certification (e.g., ACCA, ICAN).
	Strong ability to build and maintain professional relationships.
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to handle pressure, multitask, and maintain confidentiality.
	Strong computer skills and familiarity with office equipment.


Salary
N140,000 - N150,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Admin & Accounts Officer",
  "description": "HR-Aid Consults - Our client in the Telecommunication industry, is recruiting to fill the position of:&nbsp;

Job Position: Admin &amp; Accounts Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Hybrid)
Industry: Telecoms

Overview


	The Admin &amp; Accounts Officer is responsible for ensuring accurate financial record-keeping, maintaining proper cash controls, managing accounts payable and receivables, and supporting overall financial operations.
	The role also oversees general office administration, facility management, vendor relationships, and resource coordination to ensure smooth daily operations and an efficient work environment.
	This position requires a high level of integrity, attention to detail, professionalism, and the ability to&nbsp;multitask across finance and administrative functions.


Key Responsibilities
Finance &amp; Accounting:


	Ensure all financial transactions are accurately recorded, updated, and properly classified&nbsp;in the company&rsquo;s accounting system.
	Maintain data integrity in all financial reports and documentation.
	Manage accounts payable and receivable, ensuring timely settlement of obligations and&nbsp;collection of outstanding receivables.
	Make timely and accurate supplier/vendor payments.
	Record cash receipts, process bank deposits, and maintain petty cash records.
	Conduct monthly bank and account reconciliations.
	Prepare weekly financial reports and maintain financial files and records.
	Calculate and remit statutory deductions and taxes promptly (PAYE, VAT, WHT, etc.).
	Support payroll administration when required.
	Provide financial documentation and support to auditors, tax authorities, and external&nbsp;financial consultants.
	Monitor budget variances and promptly report significant discrepancies to management.


Administration &amp; Office Management:


	Oversee general office administration, ensuring smooth daily operations and a conducive&nbsp;work environment.
	Manage procurement and inventory of office supplies, equipment, and consumables.
	Tag, track, and monitor fixed assets, ensuring proper maintenance and accountability.
	Manage relationships with third parties, including vendors etc
	Ensure timely settlement of utility bills, subscriptions, and service renewals.
	Develop, implement, and manage vendor Service Level Agreements (SLAs).
	Oversee facility management activities, including office repairs, maintenance schedules,&nbsp;security, cleanliness, and fire / safety compliance.
	Ensure adequate insurance coverage for company assets and facilitate insurance claims&nbsp;where necessary.
	Support planning and coordination of team activities, meetings, and official&nbsp;correspondence.


Compliance, Controls &amp; Resource Management:


	Implement internal controls to prevent financial leakages and operational wastage.
	Ensure adherence to company policies, statutory regulations, and audit requirements.
	Maintain proper records and documentation for administrative and financial activities.
	Ensure safeguarding of company assets through proper security and disaster-prevention&nbsp;structures.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
	2&ndash;4 years of experience in accounting, administration, or a similar role.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Zoho,&nbsp;Sage).
	Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and attention to detail.
	Knowledge of tax laws, financial regulations, and basic HR administrative processes is an added advantage.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;talent@hr-aidconsults.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Admin & Accounts Officer Job at HR-Aid Consults




        
    

        
        HR-Aid Consults - Our client in the Telecommunication industry, is recruiting to fill the position of: 

Job Position: Admin & Accounts Officer

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (Hybrid)
Industry: Telecoms

Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Admin & Accounts Officer is responsible for ensuring accurate financial record-keeping, maintaining proper cash controls, managing accounts payable and receivables, and supporting overall financial operations.
	The role also oversees general office administration, facility management, vendor relationships, and resource coordination to ensure smooth daily operations and an efficient work environment.
	This position requires a high level of integrity, attention to detail, professionalism, and the ability to multitask across finance and administrative functions.


Key Responsibilities
Finance & Accounting:


	Ensure all financial transactions are accurately recorded, updated, and properly classified in the company’s accounting system.
	Maintain data integrity in all financial reports and documentation.
	Manage accounts payable and receivable, ensuring timely settlement of obligations and collection of outstanding receivables.
	Make timely and accurate supplier/vendor payments.
	Record cash receipts, process bank deposits, and maintain petty cash records.
	Conduct monthly bank and account reconciliations.
	Prepare weekly financial reports and maintain financial files and records.
	Calculate and remit statutory deductions and taxes promptly (PAYE, VAT, WHT, etc.).
	Support payroll administration when required.
	Provide financial documentation and support to auditors, tax authorities, and external financial consultants.
	Monitor budget variances and promptly report significant discrepancies to management.


Administration & Office Management:


	Oversee general office administration, ensuring smooth daily operations and a conducive work environment.
	Manage procurement and inventory of office supplies, equipment, and consumables.
	Tag, track, and monitor fixed assets, ensuring proper maintenance and accountability.
	Manage relationships with third parties, including vendors etc
	Ensure timely settlement of utility bills, subscriptions, and service renewals.
	Develop, implement, and manage vendor Service Level Agreements (SLAs).
	Oversee facility management activities, including office repairs, maintenance schedules, security, cleanliness, and fire / safety compliance.
	Ensure adequate insurance coverage for company assets and facilitate insurance claims where necessary.
	Support planning and coordination of team activities, meetings, and official correspondence.


Compliance, Controls & Resource Management:


	Implement internal controls to prevent financial leakages and operational wastage.
	Ensure adherence to company policies, statutory regulations, and audit requirements.
	Maintain proper records and documentation for administrative and financial activities.
	Ensure safeguarding of company assets through proper security and disaster-prevention structures.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field.
	2–4 years of experience in accounting, administration, or a similar role.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Zoho, Sage).
	Strong organizational, communication, and problem-solving skills.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and attention to detail.
	Knowledge of tax laws, financial regulations, and basic HR administrative processes is an added advantage.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Personal Assistant",
  "description": "Balosh Integrated Services Limited is a leading provider of access control services. Balosh is Nigeria&#39;s premier Car Parks, Highway Management, Access Control, Automatic Entrances Firm that manages parking facilities, finances the design and development of parking facilities and provides consultation to landlords with respect to all parking related matters.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Manage and maintain the Managing Director&#39;s, including scheduling meetings and appointments.
	Screen calls, emails, and correspondence, responding or redirectas appropriate.
	Organize meetings, prepare agendas, and take minutes when required.
	Coordinate travel arrangements (flights, accommodation, itineraries).
	Prepare reports, presentations, and documents.
	Maintain filing systems and ensure accurate record-keeping.
	Liaise with internal teams, clients, and external stakeholders on behalf of the MD.
	Track tasks, deadlines, and follow-ups to ensure timely execution.
	Assist with basic administrative and office management tasks.
	Run personal errands and provide ad-hoc support when needed.


Job Requirement


	Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience.
	Strong organizational and time management skills
	Excellent written and verbal communication
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook)
	Ability to multitask and prioritize effectively
	Attention to detail
	Problem-solving and proactive thinking
	Interpersonal and relationship management skills
	Proximity to Lekki Phase 1 is an added advantage
	Due to the nature of this role and specific operational requirements, preference may be given to male candidates.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@balosh.com&nbsp;using &quot;Personal Assistant&quot; &nbsp;as the subject of the email.",

Personal Assistant Job at Balosh Integrated Services Limited




        
    

        
        Balosh Integrated Services Limited is a leading provider of access control services. Balosh is Nigeria's premier Car Parks, Highway Management, Access Control, Automatic Entrances Firm that manages parking facilities, finances the design and development of parking facilities and provides consultation to landlords with respect to all parking related matters.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage and maintain the Managing Director's, including scheduling meetings and appointments.
	Screen calls, emails, and correspondence, responding or redirectas appropriate.
	Organize meetings, prepare agendas, and take minutes when required.
	Coordinate travel arrangements (flights, accommodation, itineraries).
	Prepare reports, presentations, and documents.
	Maintain filing systems and ensure accurate record-keeping.
	Liaise with internal teams, clients, and external stakeholders on behalf of the MD.
	Track tasks, deadlines, and follow-ups to ensure timely execution.
	Assist with basic administrative and office management tasks.
	Run personal errands and provide ad-hoc support when needed.


Job Requirement


	Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience.
	Strong organizational and time management skills
	Excellent written and verbal communication
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook)
	Ability to multitask and prioritize effectively
	Attention to detail
	Problem-solving and proactive thinking
	Interpersonal and relationship management skills
	Proximity to Lekki Phase 1 is an added advantage
	Due to the nature of this role and specific operational requirements, preference may be given to male candidates.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Personal Assistant"  as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Associate",
  "description": "Adler Technology is an IT enterprise solutions provider and gadget store. Our services include sales of digital gadget, enterprise IT support, digital infrastructure, custom solutions, server and storage systems, and enterprise security (CCTV, access control).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Associate

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Achieve and exceed department sales goals on a monthly, quarterly, and yearly basis
	Pitching sales proposal to potential clients

Sales Associate Job at Adler Technology

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Associate

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
